name: No-Knead Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the whole-wheat flour in a large bowl and place it in a warm oven for about 30 minutes, or until the flour and bowl reach 110°F (using a microwave for 1½ minutes).
2. In a separate container, dissolve yeast in 1 cup of warm water (110°F) and stir well.
3. Add molasses to the dissolved yeast mixture and mix thoroughly.
4. Allow the mixture to rest for about 3 minutes.
5. Pour the yeast mixture into the bowl with flour and add the remaining 3 cups of warm water, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
6. Do not knead the dough; it will be sticky.
7. Grease two large loaf pans (9" x 5" x 3") lightly with non-stick cooking spray or oil.
8. Sprinkle cornmeal on the bottom and sides of the pans to prevent sticking.
9. Use a spatula to transfer the dough into the prepared pans, smoothing the top with water-dampened hands if needed.
10. Cover the pans with a towel and place them in a warm spot.
11. Let the dough rise until it reaches the top of the pans (about 1 hour).
12. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
13. Bake for 20 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 350°F and continue baking for an additional 30-40 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
14. Carefully remove the pans from the oven and turn them onto their sides to cool on wire racks for about 15 minutes.
15. Turn the loaves out of the pans and allow them to cool completely before storing in a loaf pan or plastic bag. Store at room temperature, as the bread will be moist. If not used immediately, keep refrigerated.
WARNING: Ensure all equipment is clean and sanitized before use.
